finding_results.notes: Three interventions. (1) Vel's aphoristic 'That's what outside is for' replaced with flat 'Yeah.' — compressed acknowledgment that stays inside her practical register without producing a portable mentor line. The redirect to southern routes does the work. (2) Dren's Mira disclosure reworked from organized medical exposition into rougher bargaining speech: 'My girl's sick. Mira.' names her without staging, 'her legs —' self-interrupts before medical detail, cost arrives through trader logic ('she costs more than I've got. Every run I take is part of getting there') rather than tabulated math ('covers about half... scraping together run by run... maybe a quarter'). His indirectness about Mira is now character-true per style bible. (3) Dren's 'Lady' risk-diagnosis speech replaced with trade-pattern recognition: 'You're paying me double an eastern run to go south fast. I've been hauling cargo twenty years.' reads the customer's urgency from the price, which is how a trader identifies trouble — not through thematic pronouncement. The 'Great' speech shortened to 'So if they catch up, I'm the bastard standing next to him' — casual profanity integrated as connective tissue per Dren's profile, concrete self-assessment without elaboration.

summary: Single finding addressed across three dialogue sites. Vel's aphorism cut to a flat acknowledgment. Dren's Mira disclosure fragmented through self-interruption and trader-cost logic instead of organized medical exposition. Dren's risk assessment recast as trade-pattern reading instead of polished thematic diagnosis. All must-land beats preserved: Mira named, economic cost visible through bargaining texture, signet payoff intact. No continuity changes; heading contract preserved.
